source.title: Temu Watch 10: Logistics in times of tariffs - TechBuzz China

extractions.claim: Temu has evolved through three logistics phases: fully-managed (2022-2023), semi-managed (2024-present), and Y2/third-party model (2025)
extractions.evidence: Phase 1: Fully managed model where Temu handled all fulfillment. Phase 2: Semi-managed model launched 2024 for sellers with overseas warehouse inventory. Phase 3: Y2 model launched April 27, 2025 allowing merchants to control logistics, plus third-party platform model announced March 2025.

verification.verifier_notes: Corroborated across multiple sources including etowertech.com and 4seller.com

extractions.claim: Temu's self-operated US warehouses projected to handle 15-20% of US volume in 2025, rising to 20-25% by 2026
extractions.evidence: It is projected that by 2025, Temu's self-operated warehouses in the US will handle 15% to 20% of total US goods volume, and this proportion may increase to 20% to 25% by 2026. These self-operated warehouses primarily serve as a business buffer and for replenishing goods.

verification.verifier_notes: Industry analyst projection, not Temu official disclosure

extractions.claim: 90% of Temu's business relied on fully managed model using de minimis policy before changes
extractions.evidence: 90% of Temu's business relied on the fully managed model that used the de minimis policy. The change of the de minimis tax-free policy had a significant impact on both Temu and Shein in the North American market.

verification.verifier_notes: From industry analysis; explains urgency of logistics model pivot

extractions.claim: Temu pre-shipped 3 months of inventory to US hubs before May 2 de minimis deadline
extractions.evidence: Ahead of the May 2 end to the U.S. de minimis exemption, the company pre-shipped large volumes of popular items by sea and stocked three months of inventory in U.S. hubs.

verification.verifier_notes: From chinesellers.substack.com report on forward warehouse activation
